cards.front: **Professor Messer / Jeremy IT Lab subnetting magic table** — fill it in:
Subnet mask octet values (powers of 2 from the left):
cards.back:
```
/0  = 0
/1  = 128
/2  = 192
/3  = 224
/4  = 240
/5  = 248
/6  = 252
/7  = 254
/8  = 255
```
Same 8 values you'll ever see in a subnet mask octet (plus 0 and 255).

cards.front: Wildcard mask = inverse of subnet mask. Quick conversion table:
**DTP matrix** — outcome of each mode pair on a switch link:
cards.back:
```
                   Switch2
                acc  des  auto  trk  nego
       acc    │ acc  acc  acc  ✗MISM acc
       des    │ acc  TRK  TRK  TRK   acc
SW1    auto   │ acc  TRK  acc  TRK   acc    ← auto+auto = TRAP (stays access)
       trk    │ ✗MISM TRK TRK  TRK   TRK
       nego   │ acc  acc  acc  TRK   TRK
```
Read: TRUNK forms when ≥1 side actively wants it AND other side doesn't refuse.

```
        LACP side B
        active   passive
A: active  ✓ TRUNK  ✓ TRUNK
A: passive ✓ TRUNK  ✗ NO BUNDLE (both wait)
```
Both `passive` = same trap as DTP auto/auto. One side must be active.

```
        PAgP side B
        desirable   auto
A: desirable  ✓ BUNDLE  ✓ BUNDLE
A: auto       ✓ BUNDLE  ✗ NO BUNDLE
```
Both `auto` = no bundle. One side must be desirable.

```
Speed     | Cost
----------+-----
10 Mbps   | 100
100 Mbps  | 19
1 Gbps    | 4
10 Gbps   | 2
100 Gbps  | 1
```
Memorize all 5. Old 802.1D-1998 values: 100/19/4/2/2 (saturates at 2 above 1G).

```
Hello = 2s
Forward Delay = 15s (per state — Listening, Learning)
Max Age = 20s
```
Total convergence: 50s (MaxAge + 2× FwdDelay). 30s on new ports (skip Blocking).

```
State        | Forwards | Learns MAC | Sends BPDU | Receives BPDU
-------------+----------+------------+------------+--------------
Disabled     | no       | no         | no         | no
Blocking     | no       | no         | no         | yes
Listening    | no       | no         | yes        | yes
Learning     | no       | yes        | yes        | yes
Forwarding   | yes      | yes        | yes        | yes
```

```
Discarding   | replaces Blocking + Listening + Disabled
Learning     | learns MAC, no forward
Forwarding   | normal operation
```

```
L7-5  Application/Presentation/Session → Data
L4    Transport                        → Segment (TCP) / Datagram (UDP)
L3    Network                          → Packet
L2    Data Link                        → Frame
L1    Physical                         → Bits
```

```
Type      | Speed     | Distance | Use
----------+-----------+----------+-------------------------
Cat5      | 100 Mbps  | 100m     | legacy
Cat5e     | 1 Gbps    | 100m     | common LAN
Cat6      | 10 Gbps   | 55m      | modern LAN
Cat6a     | 10 Gbps   | 100m     | new builds, 10G to desk
Cat7/7a   | 10-40 G   | 100m     | shielded, less common
Multi-mode fiber  | 10-400G | up to ~400m | within building
Single-mode fiber | 10-400G+ | km+      | long-haul, between buildings
```

```
Standard   | Name      | Max Power | Voltage    | Common Use
-----------+-----------+-----------+------------+----------------------
802.3af    | PoE       | 15.4 W    | 44-57 V    | VoIP phone, small AP
802.3at    | PoE+      | 30 W      | 50-57 V    | 802.11n AP, PTZ camera
802.3bt T3 | PoE++     | 60 W      | 50-57 V    | 802.11ac/ax AP, video
802.3bt T4 | (also bt) | 90-100 W  | 50-57 V    | LED lighting, thin client
```

```
20/21  FTP (data/control)         | TCP
22     SSH                        | TCP
23     Telnet                     | TCP
25     SMTP                       | TCP
53     DNS                        | UDP query, TCP zone xfer
67/68  DHCP (server/client)       | UDP
69     TFTP                       | UDP
80     HTTP                       | TCP
110    POP3                       | TCP
123    NTP                        | UDP
143    IMAP                       | TCP
161/162 SNMP / SNMP-trap          | UDP
179    BGP                        | TCP
443    HTTPS                      | TCP
500    ISAKMP (IPsec)             | UDP
514    Syslog                     | UDP
520    RIP                        | UDP
546/547 DHCPv6 (client/server)    | UDP
3389   RDP                        | TCP
5060/5061 SIP (clear/TLS)         | UDP/TCP
```

```
Feature              | TCP                  | UDP
---------------------+----------------------+--------------
Connection           | Connection-oriented  | Connectionless
Handshake            | 3-way SYN/SYN-ACK/ACK| None
Reliability          | ACKs + retransmit    | Best-effort
Ordering             | Sequence numbers     | None
Flow control         | Sliding window       | None
Header size          | 20 bytes (min)       | 8 bytes
Use cases            | HTTP, SSH, FTP, SMTP | DNS, DHCP, VoIP, video
```

```
TPID (16 bits) | PCP (3) | DEI (1) | VID (12 bits)
0x8100         | CoS 0-7 | flag    | VLAN 1-4094
```
TPID 0x8100 marks the frame as VLAN-tagged. VID 12 bits = 4096 IDs (2 reserved).

```
Feature       | CDP                | LLDP
--------------+--------------------+--------------------
Vendor        | Cisco proprietary  | IEEE 802.1AB open
Default       | Often ON           | Often OFF
Hello timer   | 60s                | 30s
Hold timer    | 180s               | 120s
Multicast MAC | 0100.0ccc.cccc     | 0180.c200.000e
Used by       | Cisco gear         | Multi-vendor
```

```
Mode      | Drops | Logs (syslog) | Increments counter | Port state
----------+-------+---------------+--------------------+-----------
shutdown  | yes   | yes           | no                 | err-disabled (default)
restrict  | yes   | yes           | yes                | up
protect   | yes   | no            | no                 | up
```
